title: Large language model for post-earthquake structural damage assessment of buildings
task: 震后建筑结构损伤评估
inputData: 图像（结构损伤图片）+ 文本（自然语言问答）
contribution: 提出SDA-Chat多模态视觉问答模型，通过三阶段训练策略和跨模态高效投影层，实现震后结构损伤的自动文本化评估；问答实验最高准确率83.04%，七项联合评估总体准确率76.11%
abstract: This paper develops SDA-Chat, a multimodal vision question-answering model that automatically generates professional textual interpretations of post-earthquake structural damage images through multi-round VQA interactions. A three-stage training strategy with QLoRA fine-tuning and a novel cross-modality efficient projector are proposed to enhance VQA accuracy and inference speed across seven distinct damage assessment tasks using a dataset of 8195 image-text pairs.
methodology: SDA-Chat integrates a vision transformer (ViT) encoder, Q-former, a proposed cross-modality efficient projector (CEP), and an LLM decoder fine-tuned via QLoRA, trained through a three-stage strategy: instruction fine-tuning, image-text alignment, and domain-specific expertise learning on 8195 seismic damage image-text pairs.
results: The question-answering experiments reached a highest accuracy of 83.04%. Across seven concurrent assessment tasks, the best-performing model (LLaMA3 8B with CEP) achieved 76.11% total accuracy, with the highest single-task accuracy of 96.43% for structural material identification and the highest inference speed of 435.31 tokens/s using Phi-2.7B.
pdfFile: Jiang et al. - 2025 - Large language model for post-earthquake structural damage assessment of buildings.pdf
markdownFile: Jiang et al. - 2025 - Large language model for post-earthquake structural damage assessment of buildings.md
inputDataEn: Images (structural damage photos) + text (natural language Q&A)
